title: Inflammatory bowel disease follow-up

treatment_and_management.text: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact Crohn section supports interval symptoms, nutrition, growth where relevant, treatment context, perianal status, patient priorities, and clinician review for the Crohn subgroup without assigning activity.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting current symptoms, previous response, adverse effects, patient preferences, and clinician review context.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Reviewed separately for the ulcerative-colitis subgroup; it confirms that diagnosis-specific disease course, treatment response, adverse effects, and patient preferences cannot be collapsed into a generic IBD assumption.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current symptoms, previous response, adverse effects, patient preferences, and clinician review context.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.3 — information, treatment context, and review

follow_up.text: Supports recording patient views, relapse symptoms, agreed follow-up, access arrangements, and surveillance status.
follow_up.rationale: The exact Crohn remission section supports clinician-entered follow-up and access arrangements without setting an interval.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording patient views, relapse symptoms, agreed follow-up, access arrangements, and surveillance status.

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-reviewed monitoring results, growth, bone-health context, and communication across services.
follow_up.rationale: Reviewed for UC monitoring context; no additional generic legacy item was mapped because the workflow does not identify the IBD subtype.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed monitoring results, growth, bone-health context, and communication across services.

sources_used.title: Crohn’s disease: management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation

sources_used.title: Ulcerative colitis: management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation

source_limitations: IBD subtype, extent, activity, complications, growth, medicine, adverse effects, biomarkers, endoscopy, imaging, surveillance, surgery, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
